name: workout-dumbbell-holder-test-fork-plate-sample
description: Test print: Y-truncated fork plate (Y=+31.25 to +70) + both buttress arcs + slot profile, with 3 mm base plate. Printed inverted (fork plate bottom toward bed) to test worst-case bell-rest surface quality and slot dimensional accuracy.

dimensions.note: x = fork plate width after symmetric trim (78.5 mm). y = Y extent from +31.25 to +70 = 38.75 mm. z = fork plate (12 mm) + top buttress arc (22 mm) + bottom buttress arc (22 mm) + 3 mm base plate − overlaps = ~37 mm total from base plate bottom to top buttress top. Exact z depends on buttress overlap with fork plate geometry.

volume.note: Truncated fork plate slice + buttresses. Ribs optional. Estimated 20–28 cm3 depending on rib inclusion and exact geometry.

params.print_orientation: inverted — fork plate bottom (Z=−34 parent) toward bed

supportZones: Fork plate top face (Z=−22 parent) — now facing away from bed in inverted orientation; this is the bell-rest surface being tested
Top buttress arc underside — curved concave face, full support required
Bottom buttress arc underside — curved concave face, full support required

verification.passCriteria: Fork plate top face smooth to < 0.3 mm deviation (fingertip test). Slot opening 68.0–70.5 mm. Dumbbell shaft (D=46 mm) slides into slot and seats in arc without binding.
verification.failCriteria: Bell-rest surface has ridges > 0.3 mm that cannot be cleaned off. Slot opening out of tolerance. Shaft binds in slot.
verification.gatingDecision: FAIL on surface quality → adjust slicer support interface layer count, offset, or switch to dissolvable interface material before full print. FAIL on slot dimension → adjust saddle_radius in parent spec.json.
